diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Enamel-renal syndrome (ERS) complicated with Bartter-like syndrome and impaired renal concentration
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient was diagnosed with ERS based on the presence of amelogenesis imperfecta (AI) and bilateral nephrocalcinosis. The diagnosis of Bartter-like syndrome was supported by findings of hypokalaemia, metabolic alkalosis, high plasma renin activity, and high serum aldosterone levels. Impaired renal concentration was evidenced by low urinary osmolality despite normal serum osmolality. Genetic analysis ruled out mutations in genes associated with Bartter syndrome and nephrogenic diabetes insipidus (NDI).
